business.headline: India must pursue energy independence; fuel price cushion won't last: Gita GopinathIndia needs to speed up its shift towards energy independence as the current strategy of holding fuel prices steady becomes increasingly difficult to sustain, former IMF chief economist Gita Gopinath said, warning that the economy's present resilience could come under pressure if the Iran-US war prolongs.

general news.headline: Trump extends Iran ceasefire, seeks unified proposal as Hormuz blockade continuesUS President Donald Trump extended the Iran ceasefire after a request from Pakistan, citing appeals by Asim Munir and Shehbaz Sharif, while maintaining blockade and military readiness until a unified proposal is submitted.

The two-week ceasefire is set to end tonight.

Ahead of Pak talks, Trump's praise for Iran and a warningDonald Trump ruled out any extension to the ceasefire deadline, which will end on April 22, adding simply, "I don't want to do that. We don't have that much time."

Live: US Vice President JD Vance postpones Pakistan trip for Iran talksUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: The fragile ceasefire in the US-Israel war on Iran is nearing its deadline, with uncertainty deepening over whether diplomacy can prevent a return to conflict. US President Donald Trump has signalled he is prepared to resume military action if no agreement is reached, making clear he has little interest in extending the truce. At the same time, efforts to restart negotiations remain stalled. Iran has yet to confirm its participation in proposed talks in Pakistan, citing actions from Washington. The planned visit of Vice President JD Vance to Islamabad has been put on hold as US officials reassess their approach. Tensions have further escalated over the Strait of Hormuz, where a US naval blockade has become a central point of dispute, with Tehran viewing it as a direct breach of the ceasefire. The lack of trust between the two sides has widened in recent days. Beyond the diplomatic impasse, the wider region remains volatile. Israeli operations have continued in Lebanon despite a ceasefire. Lebanon's leadership has called for unity ahead of fresh talks with Israel in Washington. As the deadline approaches, the risk of escalation is growing if negotiations fail to materialise in time. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Indian man who lost wife, daughter in Air India crash faces deportation from UKAn Indian man who lost his wife and daughter in the 2025 Ahmedabad Air India crash has been told to leave the UK by Wednesday or face deportation. Mohammad Shethwala had been living in Britain on a dependent visa and the Home Office has now rejected his plea to extend his stay on compassionate grounds.

Donnyland in Donbas? Ukraine proposes renaming war-torn region after TrumpIn recent months of peace negotiations, Ukraine quietly floated a strikingly whimsical idea. It suggested renaming the hard-fought Donbas region Donnyland, a mix of the region's name and President Donald Trump's first name.

As the ceasefire deadline approaches, tensions are rising once again. With the clock ticking, the question now is: will there be a deal, a last-minute extension from Donald Trump, or a return to full-scale war?
